notes: works like native and the resolution problem is affecting even the windows version
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Plays fine with PS4 & Steam controllers, but with mouse input the camera stutters severely; tried disabling v-sync and using borderless window mode re similar issue https://steamcommunity.com/app/102500/discussions/0/864979375655120478/ but no good. Controlling camera with keyboard works fine. (Rating Gold as this issue does not appear to be exclusive to running with Linux/proton). Had to change resolution in personal.ini in game folder.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Runs like a greased weasel with frame-rates comparable to Windows. No graphical artifacts or audio issues although, as previously noted, resolution needs to be set in the 'personal.ini' config file (in-game attempts via the Options menu result in a crash.) Another win for Proton.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Changing brigthness crashes the game after you alt+tab/change workspaces and go back, seems like it's running borderless. About EA login – you can workaround this with xmodmap tool and rebind "@" to some other key (as well as one of your upper case letter you need in your EA password). After disabling Vsync game seems to run perfectly, but I haven't played much yet.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes:

Runs Smooth with some minor Grapahic Glitches like missing Textures etc.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Should work out of the box, to get better performance if you are on AMD or Intel based GPUs (sorry nvidia users), install galliumnine with winetricks or protontricks. The game will start with the resolution glitched a little, just change the resolution to something else, and back again and it'll fix itself (or play in windowed mode if you want)
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Ini file needs to be set to monitor resolution, otherwise the game crashes on start.

cpu: AMD A10-7700K Radeon R7, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
notes: The game will corrupt the graphics and lock up after a few hours. This happens sometimes after leaving a building.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes:
Starting with Feral Interactive's gamemode,
gamemoderun %command%

Previous comments states issues with changing resolution, I haven't noticed any issues with that.

Tabbing out of the game sometimes causes the game to have strange artifacts.

I've experienced one crash in my now, 21 hours of gameplay.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Everything works perfectly, no tweaks necessary.
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Just works out of the box! If resolution isn't good at your screen, just change personal.ini file at ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/102500/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Reckoning/personal.ini
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: I recorded the game play video with Linux kernel 5.2 and Mesa 19.1.5. Works with D9VK, see this --> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2pUxEU6swwk&feature=youtu.be&t=53
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™

notes: Using the latest version of Proton, the keyboard/mouse input did not work in game (they functioned correctly during character customization, and normal system shortcuts like alt-tab worked fine). Passing to a previous Proton version (4.2-9) solved all issues. Additionally, as many people pointed out, for changing screen resolution it is necessary to edit the "personal.ini" file (changing parameters in game results in a crash).
title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning™
